sessions.summary: Closed #83 dogfooding acceptance. Operator feared the bot wasn't helping (near-zero reactions despite team knowing it exists). Rather than shut it down on silence, sent one blunt direct ask to the team — result: 8 likes, 0 dislikes. Silence was low-effort non-response, not disinterest; bot is wanted, keep it. Acceptance evidence = organic sample of ~20 real reviews across 8 repos (mechanics all proven). Opened #127 (GEN-BUG 57%-of-findings precision audit) as a non-blocking quality follow-up. Recorded the lesson: silence is ambiguous, ask before killing.
done: Sent team a direct 'do you read the bot's comments?' ask → 8 likes / 0 dislikes
Closed #83 as accepted with the organic dogfooding sample as evidence
Opened #127 GEN-BUG precision audit as non-blocking follow-up
Saved memory lesson: low engagement is ambiguous, ask before killing
next: #127 GEN-BUG audit (low priority); #65 cron Aug 7; #100 /ask interviews
